In the current neutral regime, these structural pressures outweigh near-term defensive positioning benefits.

Rate environment favors bond alternatives over yield-compressed infrastructure.

tactical.rationale: Freight markets showing signs of stabilization after brutal 2023-2024 downcycle. Container rates have found a floor around $1,200-1,400/TEU on major routes, down from pandemic peaks but no longer in free fall. Red Sea diversions continue providing 10-15% capacity absorption. RSI at 55 suggests neutral momentum, but shipping stocks often exhibit violent reversals from oversold conditions. Recent 1.94% pop may signal early cycle turn.

positional.rationale: Above 200-day MA indicates structural support holding despite cyclical headwinds. Shipping cycles are notoriously volatile with sharp V-shaped recoveries. Current positioning below 50-day suggests market still skeptical of recovery sustainability. Historical patterns show Maersk can rally 50-100% once freight rates inflect, and technical setup suggests downside limited while upside asymmetric if trade volumes recover.

strategic.rationale: P/E at 21x for cyclical shipper indicates earnings well below peak - this is attractive entry territory, not expensive. P/B at 0.72 suggests trading below replacement cost of fleet, creating significant asset value cushion. Logistics segment (APM Terminals, supply chain solutions) likely valued at zero by market despite generating stable cash flows. Sum-of-parts analysis suggests 30-40% upside to fair value once shipping cycle normalizes. 3.1% dividend yield provides downside protection.
det_notes: AMKBY P/B 0.72: below book — value territory
HY OAS 2.71bps: tight — healthy trade environment
